title: Living in Boulder 5 - 11 - 2018: Google Earthing Boulder photos still

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a rambling update on his Google Earth historical photo project for Boulder, which he is organizing by year and is currently up to 1940. He also describes a parallel project overlaying ancient Rome's layout onto Boulder's geography based on claimed size/distance parallels, a plan to visualize Livy's histories using Google Earth screenshots, and a recent hiking recon trip tracing the Silver Lake Ditch, which he compares to Roman aqueducts.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: Silver Lake Ditch and historical water infrastructure

stances.position: amazing windows into the past that reveal the big picture when seen together
Boulder was essentially built from the blueprint of Rome, with matching sizes, distances, and infrastructure
impressive engineering comparable to Roman aqueducts, evidence Boulder settlers consciously emulated Rome
official Google Earth's Rome reconstruction is useless because it uses SketchUp and internet-dependent features; his polygon-based ground-level version is superior
